mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals an increase in aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if HOUST does not rise by 2 standard deviations over the next 5 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-percentage-point rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 60 trading days signals a decrease in labor market slack, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if UNRATE does not rise by 1 percentage point over the next 60 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) over the next 20 trading days signals a decrease in aggregate supply, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if INDPRO does not decline by 2 standard deviations over the next 20 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-percentage-point rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a 2-standard-deviation decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) over the next 60 trading days signals a decrease in labor market slack and a decrease in aggregate supply, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if UNRATE does not rise by 1 percentage point and INDPRO does not decline by 2 standard deviations over the next 60 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the next 5 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if FEDFUNDS does not rise by 2 standard deviations over the next 5 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decline in the payroll employment (PAYEMS) over the next 20 trading days signals a decrease in labor market slack, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if PAYEMS does not decline by 2 standard deviations over the next 20 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-percentage-point rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a 2-standard-deviation decline in the housing market index (HOUST) over the next 60 trading days signals a decrease in labor market slack and a decrease in aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if UNRATE does not rise by 1 percentage point and HOUST does not decline by 2 standard deviations over the next 60 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
